title: Vicoa vestita
taxonomy: Vicoa vestita belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a species of flowering plant, and its classification is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Asterales, family - Asteraceae, genus - Vicoa, species - V. vestita. This classification is based on its morphological and genetic characteristics. The family Asteraceae is one of the largest and most diverse plant families. Vicoa vestita's classification is consistent with the APG IV system, a widely accepted plant classification system.

intro: Vicoa vestita, also known as Vicoa, is a plant species belonging to the family Compositae (Asteraceae), characterized by its unique, hairy leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Vicoa vestita include sesquiterpenoid lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Vicoa vestita is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to aid in wound healing and skin issues.
